compsognathus.fact: Compsognathus was about the size of a chicken — one of the smallest dinosaurs ever found. It chased lizards and insects on its long, thin legs.

velociraptor.fact: Velociraptors were about the size of a turkey and covered in feathers. The big claw on each foot was for pinning down prey, not slashing.

pterodactyl.fact: Pterodactyls were flying reptiles, not dinosaurs. They had wings made of skin stretched from one very long finger to their body.

iguanodon.fact: Iguanodon had a spike on each thumb that scientists first thought went on its nose. It chewed plants with cheeks full of grinding teeth.

stegosaurus.fact: Stegosaurus had two rows of bony plates down its back that may have helped it warm up in the sun. Its brain was about the size of a walnut.

parasaurolophus.fact: The long tube on its head was hollow and could trumpet like a horn. Herds probably called to each other across long distances.

ankylosaurus.fact: Ankylosaurus was a tank — covered in bony plates, with a heavy club on its tail. Even its eyelids had armour.

triceratops.fact: Triceratops had three horns and a huge bony frill protecting its neck. It lived right at the very end of the age of dinosaurs.

allosaurus.fact: Allosaurus was a fierce hunter long before T. rex appeared. It opened its mouth wider than almost any other dinosaur and bit like an axe.

spinosaurus.fact: Spinosaurus had a huge sail on its back and was probably the only dinosaur that mostly lived in water, hunting fish like a giant crocodile.

brachiosaurus.fact: Brachiosaurus held its neck high like a giraffe and could reach the tops of trees. It might have weighed more than ten elephants put together.

trex.fact: T. rex had the strongest bite of any land animal that ever lived. Its tiny arms were still strong enough to lift a grown person.
